arts.ttl: Roma CEO Lina Souloukou resigns amid turmoil
arts.sum: In a shocking development at AS Roma, CEO Lina Souloukou has submitted her resignation on this morning. This news comes on the heels of recent events that saw Souloukou and her children placed under police protection due to threats received following the dismissal of manager Daniele De Rossi. The club released an official statement acknowledging Souloukou’s departure: “AS Roma announces that CEO Lina Souloukou has resigned. We thank Lina for her dedication during a particularly critical phase for the Club and wish her the best in her future professional challenges. arts.ttl: Serie A: Roma vs Udinese – probable line-ups and where to watch on TV
arts.sum: Matias Soulé is expected to start on the bench again in Ivan Juric’s first game...

Roma have won 18 of their last 22 games against Udinese in Serie A (D1 L3) – since the 2013/14 season, the Giallorossi have won more games (18) and kept more clean sheets (11) against this opponent than any other. Roma have scored 182 goals in their 100 previous meetings with Udinese in Serie A: an average of 1.82 goals per game - the highest for the Giallorossi against any opponent they’ve faced more than 60 times in the top-flight.

arts.ttl: Roma CEO Under Police Protection Amid Fan Backlash
arts.sum: These are delicate times for Roma CEO Lina Souloukou after provoking fans' anger with the recent dismissal of Daniele De Rossi. According to La Repubblica, fears over Souloukou's safety have convinced local authorities to place her under police protection.
